parts.text: "Project @onebusaway-gtfs-modules/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @onebusaway-gtfs-modules/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency. (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @onebusaway-gtfs-modules/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

I need to check for compilation failures, possibly caused by an updated dependency that doesn't match the current build. There's a chance a changed API in Hibernate 5.4.24 has affected the build, like the `buildSessionFactory` method. I should inspect the documentation and the Javadocs folder. It seems there might be a breaking change from 5.3 to 5.4.24, especially with `ServiceRegistryBuilder`. The user mentioned their project isn't compiling, so I need to ensure my local build reflects any necessary updates. Checking the POM for dependency versions definitely sounds like a good idea!

The project currently builds, which suggests the breakage is version-specific rather than present in the checkout. I’m checking the Hibernate module POM and the 5.4.24 Javadocs for the APIs that disappeared or changed shape, especially `Configuration`/`ServiceRegistryBuilder`/query signatures.
